name: South Newton
overview: South Newton is a village and civil parish on the A36 about 4 to 5 miles north-west of Salisbury, on the left bank of the River Wylye between chalk downs and the ancient Grovely Wood. The parish, which also takes in the village of Stoford and the hamlets of Little Wishford and Chilhampton, had a population of 819 at the 2011 census. There has been a settlement here since Saxon times, and the parish church of St Andrew has medieval origins.
character: A linear Wylye-valley village set along and around the A36, with riverside water meadows on one side and downland and woodland on the other.
amenities: The Swan Inn riverside pub at neighbouring Stoford (check current opening, as it has periodically been closed)
Bus stop on the A36 with services to Salisbury and Wilton
No village shop - everyday shopping is in Wilton or Salisbury

commutes.notes: About 5 to 6 miles south-east via the A36; Wilton is closer still
From Salisbury (about 6 miles) on South Western Railway
Buses run along the A36 between Warminster, Wilton and Salisbury

prices.summary: Housing is a mix of period cottages and later 20th-century and modern homes straddling the A36, with riverside positions attracting a premium; frontage to the trunk road tempers values for some properties. See the live Rightmove sold-price page for current figures.

thingsToDo: Walking in ancient Grovely Wood and along its Roman road byway
The Hadden Hill loop walk from South Newton through the woods towards Wilton
Fly-fishing and riverside strolls along the River Wylye
Visiting nearby Wilton (Wilton House) and Stonehenge
placesToEat: The Swan Inn at Stoford (riverside coaching inn)
Pubs and restaurants in nearby Wilton
pros: Attractive Wylye-valley and Grovely Wood setting with excellent walking
Only about 5 to 6 miles from Salisbury, with Wilton closer still
Riverside character on the chalk-stream Wylye
cons: The A36 trunk road runs through the village, bringing traffic and noise
No village shop or school in South Newton itself
whoItSuits: Walkers and countryside lovers wanting a Wylye-valley village close to both Wilton and Salisbury, who can live with the A36 nearby.

sources.label: British History Online - VCH Wiltshire, South Newton
H&W White - South Newton and Stoford (Wylye Valley)
